name: MicrosoftSqlDeployment
description: Deploy SQL Server, Azure SQL Database, or Azure SQL Managed Instance using DACPAC, SQL scripts, or SQL projects
author: Microsoft Corporation

inputs.name: action
inputs.label: Action
inputs.helpMarkDown: The SqlPackage action to run, or sqlScript to execute a .sql file against the target database.

inputs.name: path
inputs.label: Path
inputs.helpMarkDown: Path to a .dacpac, .sqlproj, or .sql file. The file type is detected automatically from the extension.

inputs.name: connectionString
inputs.label: Connection String
inputs.helpMarkDown: Connection string for the target database. Store it as a secret pipeline variable and reference it here (e.g. $(ConnectionString)) to avoid exposing credentials in plain text.

inputs.name: azureSubscription
inputs.label: Azure Subscription
inputs.helpMarkDown: Azure Resource Manager service connection used for firewall rule management and Entra ID authentication.

inputs.name: publishProfile
inputs.label: Publish Profile
inputs.helpMarkDown: Path to a SqlPackage publish profile (.xml file). Any values set in additionalArguments take precedence over the profile.

inputs.name: additionalArguments
inputs.label: Additional Arguments
inputs.helpMarkDown: Additional arguments appended to the SqlPackage or sqlcmd command.

inputs.name: buildArguments
inputs.label: Build Arguments
inputs.helpMarkDown: Additional arguments passed to dotnet build when the path points to a .sqlproj file.

inputs.name: sqlpackagePath
inputs.label: SqlPackage Path
inputs.helpMarkDown: Path to the SqlPackage executable. When not specified, the task searches via dotnet tool install, DacFramework MSI (Windows only), then PATH.

inputs.name: sqlcmdPath
inputs.label: Sqlcmd Path
inputs.helpMarkDown: Path to the sqlcmd executable. When not specified, the task uses sqlcmd from PATH or auto-installs go-sqlcmd.

inputs.name: firewallRuleManagement
inputs.label: Firewall Rule Management
inputs.helpMarkDown: When enabled, the task temporarily adds a firewall rule for the agent IP and removes it after deployment. Defaults to true when azureSubscription is provided. Disable this for private VNet or on-premises deployments.

outputVariables.name: SqlDeploymentOutputFile
outputVariables.description: Path to generated output file for script, deployReport, driftReport, extract, export actions

messages.SqlPackageNotFound: SqlPackage not found. Install via: dotnet tool install -g microsoft.sqlpackage
messages.SqlPackageNotFoundAtPath: SqlPackage not found at specified path: %s. Ensure the path points to a valid SqlPackage executable.
messages.SqlCmdInstalling: sqlcmd not found on PATH. Installing go-sqlcmd...
messages.SqlcmdNotFoundAtPath: sqlcmd not found at specified path: %s. Ensure the path points to a valid sqlcmd executable.
messages.SqlcmdAutoInstallFailed: Failed to auto-install go-sqlcmd: %s. Install sqlcmd manually or specify the sqlcmdPath input.
messages.SqlcmdUnsupportedPlatform: Unsupported platform for sqlcmd auto-install: %s. Supported platforms: win32, linux, darwin.
messages.SqlcmdExecutableNotFoundAfterExtract: sqlcmd executable not found after extraction at: %s.
messages.ConnectionSuccessful: Connection successful - no firewall rule needed
messages.AddingFirewallRule: Adding temporary firewall rule for IP: %s
messages.FirewallManagementRequiresAzureSubscript: Firewall rule management requires azureSubscription input
messages.ClientHasAccessToServer: Client has access to SQL server. Skip adding firewall exception.
messages.FailedToDetectIPAddress: Failed to detect client IP address. Connection error: %s
messages.ConnectionFailedFirewallDisabled: Connection failed. If deploying to Azure SQL, ensure your agent has network access or set firewallRuleManagement to true.
messages.NoSQLServersFound: No SQL servers found in subscription: %s
messages.SQLServerNotFoundInSubscription: SQL Server '%s' not found in subscription: %s
messages.FailedToGetSQLServerDetails: Failed to get SQL Server '%s' details: %s
messages.InvalidConnectionString: Invalid connection string. A valid connection string is a series of keyword/value pairs separated by semi-colons. If there are any special characters like quotes or semi-colons in the keyword value, enclose the value within quotes. Refer to https://aka.ms/sqlconnectionstring for more info.
messages.UnsupportedAuthentication: Unsupported Authentication type: '%s'. Supported values: SQL auth (no Authentication keyword), Active Directory Default, Active Directory Password, Active Directory Service Principal, Active Directory Integrated.
messages.ConnectionStringMissingServer: Invalid connection string. Please ensure 'Server' or 'Data Source' is provided in the connection string.
messages.ConnectionStringMissingDatabase: Invalid connection string. Please ensure 'Database' or 'Initial Catalog' is provided in the connection string.
messages.ConnectionStringMissingUserId: Invalid connection string. Please ensure 'User' or 'User ID' is provided in the connection string.
messages.ConnectionStringMissingPassword: Invalid connection string. Please ensure 'Password' is provided in the connection string.
messages.ConnectionStringMissingClientId: Invalid connection string. Please ensure client ID is provided in the 'User' or 'User ID' field of the connection string.
messages.ConnectionStringMissingClientSecret: Invalid connection string. Please ensure client secret is provided in the 'Password' field of the connection string.
messages.DotnetNotFound: .NET SDK not found. Install via UseDotNet@2 task.
messages.DacpacNotFoundAfterBuild: Built .dacpac not found at expected path: %s
messages.SqlProjectBuildFailed: SQL project build failed. Exit code: %s. Error: %s
messages.AccessTokenAcquired: Access token acquired from service connection for database authentication.
messages.SqlPackageExecutionFailed: SqlPackage execution failed with exit code: %s
messages.InvalidAction: Invalid action: %s. Supported actions: publish, script, deployReport.
